items.title: Cranking Up The Inflation Machine: Fed Delivers Rate Cut
items.description: The FOMC voted 11-1 to cut the federal funds rate by a quarter percent, setting the interest rate between 4 and 4.25 percent.

items.title: This Fed Meeting Must Have Been A Circus
items.description: The FOMC voted today to cut the Fedâs five policy rates by 25 basis points, the first cut in 2025, after cutting by 100 basis points in 2024.

items.title: Fed Watch: Will It Be DéJà Vu All Over Again?
items.description: With the Fed resuming rate cuts at its September FOMC meeting, investors are now watching closely to see if 2025 will mirror last yearâs end-of-year easing cycle.

items.title: Federal Reserve Resumes Rate Cuts, Extends Inflation Runway
items.description: The Federal Reserve cut rates by 25 basis points and projects gradual reductions through 2028, with a long-term target of 3%. Read what investors need to know.

items.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Bought 2 Stocks In 2025 — One's Already A Meme Favorite
items.description: FBI Director Kash Patel was asked about two stocks he bought earlier this year by a member of Congress on Wednesday and gave a surprising answer. The transactions come as members of Congress are working on legislation to ban lawmakers and cabinet members from buying and selling stocks.
